label: Graph search, BFS/DFS & A*

node.label: Graph search, BFS/DFS & A*
node.definition: Graph search explores state transitions with breadth-first, depth-first, uniform-cost, or heuristic A* strategies while tracking visited states and path cost.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Graph search, BFS/DFS & A* is modeled as depending on Algorithms & data structures, Graph theory and feeding downstream nodes such as Monte Carlo tree search, Program synthesis & code search, Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning.
examples: atlas downstream link to Monte Carlo tree search
atlas downstream link to Program synthesis & code search
atlas downstream link to Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning
limitations: Search and self-evaluation can multiply cost while preserving confident errors when states, tools, or verifiers are weak. Its direct dependencies include Algorithms & data structures, Graph theory.

dependencies.rationale: Graph search, BFS/DFS & A* explores alternatives using the state, scoring, or search process in Algorithms & data structures.
Graph search, BFS/DFS & A* explores alternatives using the state, scoring, or search process in Graph theory.
